visual_description: A man in a dark suit and tie stands in a dimly lit office, holding a phone to his ear, while other individuals in business attire move around or sit at desks with computer monitors. Behind him, a wall displays multiple framed photographs, and a person in a blue shirt with “OCTB” on the back walks past. The man’s gaze is directed forward, and his posture is upright, suggesting attentiveness to his phone conversation.
audio_description: The audio clip begins with a faint, high-frequency hiss, indicating a low-fidelity recording environment. A male voice, speaking in standard Mandarin with a neutral accent, calmly and clearly says, “你挂机啊” (“You’re on hold.”), his tone measured and slightly exasperated. Immediately following, he continues, “你不知道转个频道” (“Don’t you know how to switch the channel?”), maintaining a steady, conversational delivery. The background features a continuous, low-volume, synthesized string pad, reminiscent of 1980s or early 1990s electronic music, which adds a somber, suspenseful atmosphere. A second male voice, deeper and more forceful, interrupts with a sharp, clipped tone: “说完了没有？” (“Are you done talking?”). His delivery is abrupt and authoritative, suggesting a position of power or urgency. The first speaker, now more hurried and, responds with, “四五六八五五” (“456855”), a sequence of numbers spoken rapidly and without pause, as if reciting a code or password. The second speaker, still dominant, sharply commands, “还说！” (“Still talking!”), his voice rising in volume and intensity
